text: The δ-GLMB filter for tracking multiple targets was recently derived as a solution to the multi-target Bayes recursion. This work focuses on developing efficient implementations of this new filter. Without specific approximations, the standard implementation of the δ-GLMB filter involves filtering and prediction steps, both of which involve computing weighted sums of multi-target exponentials with an unmanageably large number of terms. In this paper, we develop an efficient implementation that uses ranked assignment and K-shortest paths techniques to prune the sums and keep only a few of the largest terms. In addition, we developed look-ahead PHD filter implementations to further accelerate the computations. We derive a mathematical characterization of the L1-error that is introduced to the multi-target distribution as a result of pruning.
